headline: The FBI and CISA warned that Russian intelligence now phishes Signal Backup Recovery Keys to reopen victims' entire message histories, while attackers increasingly turn AI coding agents and fake OpenAI tenants into weapons.

emerging_trends.text: Attackers keep turning AI assistants against the people who run them, hiding payloads inside clean-looking GitHub repositories that coding agents execute on sight and standing up fake OpenAI tenants to phish employees.
emerging_trends.topic_title: AI Coding Agents Tricked Into Running Malware

emerging_trends.text: Russian intelligence sharpened its long-running phishing of Signal users, now extracting Backup Recovery Keys that reopen entire message histories and survive a victim's attempt to lock the attacker out.
emerging_trends.topic_title: Russian Hackers Steal Signal Backup Recovery Keys

emerging_trends.text: Defenders are confronting the long tail of code they no longer control, from aging end-of-life open source components to third-party vendors whose breaches reach prediction markets, password managers, and schools.
emerging_trends.topic_title: Initiative Targets End-of-Life Open Source Risk

topics.title: AI Coding Agents Tricked Into Running Malware
topics.latest_developments: BleepingComputer reported on June 27 that a benign-looking GitHub repository can drive an agentic coding tool to execute a hidden payload that stays invisible to security scanners, the AI agent, and human reviewers, while separately threat actors spun up fraudulent OpenAI tenants impersonating real companies to lure employees into leaking sensitive data in chats and projects.
topics.summary: As developers hand setup and research tasks to autonomous AI assistants, attackers weaponize the tools' trust, planting executable traps in repositories and faking OpenAI organization invites; teams should treat agent-run code and unsolicited tenant invites as untrusted.

sources.title: Clean GitHub repo tricks AI coding agents into running malware
Cybersecurity firms targeted by fraudulent OpenAI organization invites

topics.title: Russian Hackers Steal Signal Backup Recovery Keys
topics.latest_developments: The FBI and CISA updated their March 2026 warning on June 26, revealing the Russian intelligence phishing campaign now coaxes Signal users into surrendering their Backup Recovery Key, which lets attackers restore the account's backup, read its full private and group history, and keep that access even after the victim notices.
topics.summary: Operators tied to Russian intelligence services run targeted phishing against Signal accounts; once a victim hands over the recovery key it keeps working, so the FBI and CISA urge users to rotate keys and verify linked devices.

sources.title: FBI: Russian hackers now target Signal backup recovery keys
FBI Warns Russian Intelligence Hackers Target Signal Backup Recovery Keys

topics.title: Cisco Buys Astrix and WideField for Non-Human Identity
topics.latest_developments: Dark Reading reported that Cisco acquired Astrix and WideField to fold non-human identity into its security stack, betting that securing the agentic workforce means making identity the primary control plane.
topics.summary: As autonomous agents and machine identities multiply, Cisco joins the vendors treating non-human identity as the core control; the Astrix and WideField deals aim to govern and secure those credentials.

sources.title: Cisco Adds NHI to Security Stack With Astrix, WideField Acquisitions

topics.title: Cisco Unified CM Flaw Under Active Exploitation
topics.latest_developments: CISA on June 26 gave federal agencies until Sunday, June 28, to patch CVE-2026-20230, the server-side request forgery flaw in Cisco Unified Communications Manager that attackers are now exploiting in the wild.
topics.summary: CVE-2026-20230 is a high-severity SSRF bug in Cisco's enterprise call-management server; with active exploitation and a binding CISA deadline, administrators should apply Cisco's fix immediately.

sources.title: CISA sets urgent deadline to fix Cisco flaw exploited in attacks

topics.title: Pentagon Probes Dialog Data Exposure
topics.latest_developments: Wired reported on June 26 that the Pentagon is investigating a Dialog data exposure whose leaked records unmasked a senior White House intelligence official and an active-duty special operations officer.
topics.summary: Exposed records from the private Dialog group included personal information on national security personnel; the Defense Department is examining how the leak could unmask officials who depend on protected identities.

sources.title: The Pentagon Is Looking Into the Dialog Data Exposure for Unmasking National Security Officials

topics.title: Initiative Targets End-of-Life Open Source Risk
topics.latest_developments: Dark Reading detailed the new Open Source Sustainability Initiative, whose goal is to help enterprises manage and secure aging, end-of-life open source projects while staying compliant with regulations.
topics.summary: Abandoned open source components keep running deep inside enterprise software; the Open Source Sustainability Initiative aims to give companies a way to track and secure those aging dependencies.

sources.title: New Initiative Tackles Security for End-of-Life Open Source Software

weather: This Afternoon: Showers And Thunderstorms, high 79F.
Tonight: Showers And Thunderstorms Likely then Patchy Fog, low 66F.
Sunday: Partly Sunny then Chance Showers And Thunderstorms, high 85F.

games.recap: Marte's tiebreaking homer in 8th after 4-run inning against Skenes helps Reds beat Pirates 6-4

business_politics.title: Insurance Rulemaker Suspends Risk Ratings After Cyberattack
business_politics.latest_developments: The National Association of Insurance Commissioners suspended its investment risk designations on June 27 after a cyberattack disabled the system.
business_politics.summary: The National Association of Insurance Commissioners assigns the risk ratings that decide how much capital U.S. life and property insurers must hold against their bond and asset portfolios; the suspension leaves carriers and their regulators without that benchmark while the association investigates the intrusion.

sources.title: US insurance rulemaker suspends investment risk designations after cyber attack

business.title: Highmark Wholecare Hands Out Free Produce
business.latest_developments: Highmark Wholecare gave away 8,000 pounds of produce at a free farmers market June 27, with the next event set for July 8.
business.summary: Highmark Wholecare distributed 8,000 pounds of produce to families at a free farmers market and will hold the next one July 8 from noon to 3 p.m. at Duquesne City School, part of a summer series across the region.

sources.title: Highmark Wholecare gives out 8,000 pounds of produce to families at free farmers market

business.title: SanaView Farms Winery Draws Visitors to Laurel Highlands
business.latest_developments: TribLive profiled SanaView Farms Winery & Cafe on June 27 as word of its all-natural wines spreads.
business.summary: SanaView Farms Winery & Cafe, a rural farm operation in the Laurel Highlands, pours all-natural wines and serves Italian fare, drawing visitors such as Tom and Evonne Cunningham who sought out the buzz.

sources.title: SanaView Farms Winery & Cafe boasts natural wines, Italian fare

around_town.title: Lawsuit Says Trump Title X Rule Threatens Pa. Family Planning
around_town.latest_developments: A suit against the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services, reported June 27, says a new Title X application step could delay or deny funding for tens of thousands of Pennsylvanians.
around_town.summary: The Family Health Council of Central Pennsylvania, based in Cumberland County, joined a lawsuit alleging that a step the Department of Health and Human Services added to Title X grant applications this year jeopardizes federally funded reproductive and family-planning care for about 160,000 Pennsylvania patients.

sources.title: Trump admin rule puts reproductive health care for 160K Pa. patients at risk, lawsuit says

around_town.title: South Side Street Fest Adds Security for Second Weekend
around_town.latest_developments: Organizers added metal detectors, ID scanners, and a separate locals' line for the festival's second weekend after long lines marred opening night.
around_town.summary: The South Side Street Fest returned for a second weekend with extra metal detectors, ID scanners, and a dedicated line for locals to speed entry; an organizer said Saturday's larger crowds would be the real test of the changes.

sources.title: South Side Street Fest adds more metal detectors and ID scanner to speed up lines

events.title: Noah Kahan, Benson Boone, Dave Matthews Lead July Concerts
events.latest_developments: TribLive's July concert preview, published June 27, leads with a sold-out Noah Kahan show at PNC Park.
events.summary: Pittsburgh's July concert calendar features a sold-out Noah Kahan date at PNC Park, a near-capacity arena show by Benson Boone, and the return of summer staple Dave Matthews Band.

sources.title: Top July concerts for Pittsburgh: Noah Kahan, Benson Boone, Dave Matthews Band

events.title: Starfire Powers Pittsburgh's July 4 Fireworks
events.latest_developments: The Post-Gazette detailed June 27 how Cambria County's Starfire will stage Pittsburgh's Fourth of July fireworks.
events.summary: Pittsburgh's July 4 fireworks come from Starfire, a Cambria County pyrotechnics company the Post-Gazette profiled for the artistry behind the city's self-described 'very noisy' Independence Day show.

sources.title: 'We're a very noisy group': Pittsburgh's July 4 fireworks are powered by the artistry of Cambria County's Starfire

around_teams.title: Pirates Activate Konnor Griffin From the IL
around_teams.latest_developments: The Pirates activated top prospect Konnor Griffin from the injured list June 26, days after his rehab work at Double-A Altoona.
around_teams.summary: The Pittsburgh Pirates brought infield prospect Konnor Griffin back from the injured list, adding reinforcements to a roster thinned by injuries; he homered leading off in his return, the Post-Gazette reported.

sources.title: Pirates activate Konnor Griffin from the IL, providing much-needed reinforcements

around_teams.title: Pirates Prospects Enter Baseball's Labor Fight
around_teams.latest_developments: A Post-Gazette 'Off The Bat' column June 27 argued the players' union can cite Konnor Griffin and Endy Rodriguez in collective-bargaining disputes.
around_teams.summary: The Post-Gazette's 'Off The Bat' contends the Major League Baseball Players Association can point to the Pirates' handling of Konnor Griffin, Endy Rodriguez, and ace Paul Skenes as evidence in service-time and pay fights heading into the next collective bargaining agreement.

sources.title: Off The Bat: Why the MLBPA can cite Konnor Griffin and Endy Rodriguez in the latest CBA debates

around_teams.title: Eric Ebron Talks Retirement on 'Not Just Football'
around_teams.latest_developments: On Cam Heyward's 'Not Just Football,' former Steelers tight end Eric Ebron discussed retiring at 28 and the 2020 11-0 Steelers team.
around_teams.summary: Former tight end Eric Ebron joined Cam Heyward's 'Not Just Football' podcast to walk through his NFL run across Detroit, Indianapolis, and Pittsburgh, his retirement at 28, and Bill Belichick's move to coach North Carolina.

sources.source: Not Just Football with Cam Heyward
sources.title: Eric Ebron Talks Andrew Luck's Retirement, The 11-0 Steelers Team, & More! | Not Just Football

team_usa.title: U.S. Eyes a Knockout Path Through Europe's Best
team_usa.latest_developments: With the group stage closing June 27, analysts mapped a U.S. title path that could require beating Spain, France, and England.
team_usa.summary: Mauricio Pochettino's United States won Group D and enters the round of 32 as a World Cup co-host; the Guardian projected that a run to the title could pit the Americans against Spain, France, and England, three of the tournament's strongest sides.

sources.title: Who would the US need to beat to win the World Cup? Probably only Spain, France and England

team_usa.title: Ranch Dressing Becomes a U.S. World Cup Calling Card
team_usa.latest_developments: ESPN documented June 27 how visiting fans embraced Weston McKennie's love of ranch dressing as a U.S. World Cup motif.
team_usa.summary: American midfielder Weston McKennie's fondness for ranch dressing, once mocked, has spread among international visitors at the U.S.-hosted World Cup, turning the condiment into a viral emblem of the home side.

sources.title: Not laughing now: How World Cup fans vindicated McKennie

reading.author: Ed Zitron
reading.title: Cargo Culture
reading.summary: Zitron argues that much of today's AI industry runs on cargo-cult imitation, copying the rituals and rhetoric of past tech successes without the underlying economics to sustain the spending.

reading.author: Stratechery
reading.title: An Interview with Figma CEO Dylan Field About Design and AI
reading.summary: Ben Thompson interviews Figma chief executive Dylan Field on building the company and why Field sees AI as a tailwind for design software rather than a threat.

reading.author: Cal Newport
reading.title: Dear AI Companies: Stop the "Doom Trolling"
reading.summary: Newport argues AI companies erode trust by loudly warning that their own products are dangerously powerful, likening the tactic to a carmaker fear-mongering about its own trucks to sell them.

sports_media: Not Just Football with Cam Heyward
